title: codonoprasum oleraceum
intro: Codonoprasum oleraceum, also known as wild parsley or wild chervil, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of codonoprasum oleraceum are attributed to its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of codonoprasum oleraceum can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
